title: Parent Support Groups * Substance Use Disorder Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parent Support Groups * Substance Use Disorder Issues 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Mothers Perinatal Depression Substance Use Disorder Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in space for social activities. Offers assistance with resume building, help to quit smoking, and access to support groups for substance use issues and parenting. 155 S Empire Boulevard Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Individuals and families with minor children in substance use disorder recovery Hours: Monday/Thursday 8am-8pm, Tuesday-Wednesday/Friday 8am-5pm, Saturday 1pm-4pm, Sunday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 756-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES ABC PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer mentoring to parents impacted by addiction who are involved in ODHS Child Welfare and criminal justice system. Parents are matched with a mentor who helps them stay on track, work through child welfare system and complete substance abuse treatment. Mentors help connect parents to recovery communities. The program is a part of Parents Anonymous Oregon. 7820 NE Holman Street Suite B5 Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Available to any parent who: -Has an open Child Welfare case due to substance abuse -Is currently in treatment for substance abuse -Is reuniting with their children after successful treatment Hours: Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-4590 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES SALEM PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer mentoring to parents impacted by addiction who are involved in ODHS Child Welfare and criminal justice system. Parents are matched with a mentor who helps them stay on track, work through child welfare system and complete substance abuse treatment. Mentors help connect parents to recovery communities. The program is a part of Parents Anonymous Oregon. 3857 Wolverine Street NE Suite 22 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Available to any parent who: -Has an open Child Welfare case due to substance abuse -Is currently in treatment for substance abuse -Is reuniting with their children after successful treatment Hours: Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-4685 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM
